data.title: Warsh testimony: Traders bet on themes around inflation, rates, and AI
data.description:
Kevin Warsh, nominated by Donald Trump to lead the Federal Reserve, is set to testify before the Senate Banking Committee, with markets closely watching for policy signals.

Investors are focused less on confirmation odds and more on the substance of Warsh’s remarks, particularly around inflation trends, the timing of potential rate cuts, and the economic implications of artificial intelligence. Prediction market platform Kalshi indicates traders expect these themes to dominate the hearing. 

The testimony could provide insight into how Warsh might steer monetary policy if confirmed, offering markets a clearer sense of the Federal Reserve’s future direction. Outlined below are what traders on Kalshi are pricing in to what Warsh will say during the hearing: 

  • Inflation — 98% chance.
  • Trump — 98% chance.
  • Wife — 96% chance.
  • 2008 / Financial Crisis — 94% chance.
  • Powell — 94% chance.
  • AI / Artificial Intelligence — 89% chance.
  • Crypto / Cryptocurrency / Bitcoin — 86% chance. 
  • Rate Cut / Cut Rate — 85% chance. 
  • Tariff — 84% chance.
  • Oil — 84% chance. 
  • Bessent — 49% chance. 
  • Iran — 49% chance.
  • Estee Lauder — 46% chance. 
  • Cook — 30% chance.
  • Renovation — 26% chance. 
  • Epstein — 18% chance.
  • Judy Shelton — 4% chance. 

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: Intel's former CEO joins board of Australian semiconductor startup Syenta
data.description:
Intel's (INTC) former CEO Pat Gelsinger is joining the board of directors for Syenta, an Australia-based semiconductor company focused on building advanced packaging technology for artificial intelligence systems.

Syenta recently completed a Series A funding round of $26M. The round was led by Playground Global, where Gelsinger serves as a general partner. Australia's National Reconstruction Fund co-led with participation from Investible, Salus Ventures, Jelix Ventures, and Wollemi Capital. The funds are intended to help Syenta move towards commercialization.

The company, headquartered in Sydney, has developed a technology called Localized Electrochemical Manufacturing, which enables high-density chip-to-chip interconnects within existing semiconductor manufacturing infrastructure. This is supposed to shorten the advanced packaging process for AI systems and help alleviate supply chain constraints across the industry. The technology is also intended to improve the efficiency of how chips connect and communicate.

Syenta said it can lead to a 40% reduction in process steps, requires no redesign for existing fabrication infrastructure, and improves the bandwidth between chips.

"This is a new way of building high-performance systems at unprecedented scale and power, particularly as AI workloads continue to grow," Gelsinger said. "AI's next scaling challenge isn’t just compute—it's how chips connect. Syenta is addressing a fundamental constraint in advanced packaging, and that has the potential to reshape how future systems are built and scaled."

As part of its commercialization strategy, Syenta is establishing an office in Arizona, located near facilities operated by Intel and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM).

"There's this connected part between cheap chiplets that currently are not keeping up with the demand in AI compute," said Syenta co-founder and CEO Jeka Viktorova in an interview last year with Forbes Australia. "Our method accelerates this connection, and we're able to build the parts that connect it to chiplets much faster than conventional technology."

Viktorova said their solution will help overcome a bottleneck in advanced packaging and make AI and quantum chips more efficient.

data.title: Watch these stocks after the retail sales report came in strong
data.description:
Retail sales shot up in March as tax refunds boosted consumer spending, with an easy comparison to a year ago when tariff concerns weighed. While the March increase in retail sales was led by a record jump in spending on gas, almost every category notched an increase. 

Retail sales climbed 1.7% M/M to $752.1B in March, topping the +1.4% consensus and accelerating from the +0.7% pace in February, according to advance data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Tuesday. Better yet, the February increase in retail sales was revised up from the +0.6% rate initially reported.

The electronics & appliance stores category (+0.9% M/M, +5.2% Y/Y) had a strong month, which bodes well for both Best Buy (BBY) and GameStop (GME).

The nonstore retailers category (+1.0% M/M, +10.1% Y/Y) shined once again. Amazon (AMZN) is the most important name in the category, although the strong showing could also be a positive for Bath & Body Works (BBWI), Etsy (ETSY), and Wayfair (W).

The building material and garden equipment category (+0.7% M/M, +2.6% Y/Y) also had a strong month in a read that is usually positive for Home Depot (HD), Lowe's (LOW), Tractor Supply (TSCO), Floor & Decor (FND), and Fastenal (FAST).

"Higher gasoline prices amid the Iran war drove the headline gains in March retail sales. Even with consumers forced to allocate more of their budgets to energy, overall demand held up better than expected, likely reflecting larger tax refunds and continued resilience among higher-income households. The picture is less sanguine once we account for inflation, however," updated the Bloomberg economics team.

"Higher gas prices are taking a bite, but the damage is limited. While these numbers are good news for the economy overall, they don’t necessarily support rate cuts by the Federal Reserve," updated David Russell, global head of market strategy at TradeStation.

data.title: Wall Street rises as investors renew hopes of a U.S.-Iran truce, Warsh faces confirmation hearing
data.description:
U.S. stocks were higher on Tuesday as investors hope for a peace deal between Iran and the U.S. while former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh started his confirmation hearings in the Senate.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.3%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.4%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.7%.

Over in the bond market, the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 3 basis points higher at 4.28%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose 4 basis points to 3.77%.

U.S. President Donald Trump told CNBC that he expects the U.S. to “end up with a great deal” with Iran to end the war. He also said he does not expect to extend the ongoing ceasefire, which will expire on Wednesday. “I think they have no choice,” he said.

Vice President JD Vance, along with the U.S. delegation, is expected to land in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Wednesday for another round of talks, CNN reported.

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) were lower at $87, while Brent (CO1:COM) also slid to $95 per barrel.

Kevin Warsh, the former Fed governor whom President Donald Trump nominated as Federal Reserve chair, will face a barrage of questions on Tuesday during his confirmation hearings in the Senate. The confirmation hearing starts at 10 a.m. ET.

On the U.S. economy, retail sales jumped more than expected in March.

Pending home sales jumped 1.5% month-over-month in March, higher than consensus, the National Association of Realtors said. Moreover, February business inventories rose +0.4% MoM to $2,686.8B vs. +0.2% consensus, according to the U.S. Census Bureau.

data.title: Business inventories rise more than expected in February
data.description:
Business inventories increased 0.4% M/M to $2,686.8B vs. +0.2% consensus and 0.0% prior (revised from -0.1%), according to data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Tuesday.

The combined value of distributive trade sales and manufacturers' shipments for February was estimated at $2,014.8B, up 1.7% from January and up 5.5% from February 2025.

The total business inventories/sales ratio based on seasonally adjusted data at the end of February was 1.33, vs. 1.35 in January and 1.39 in February 2025, the Census Bureau said.

data.title: Pending home sales rise more than expected in March
data.description:
The U.S. Pending Home Sales Index jumped 1.5% M/M to 73.7 in March, compared to the +0.9% consensus and the 2.5% advance recorded in February (revised from 1.8%), according to data released by the National Association of Realtors on Tuesday.

Pending transactions fell 1.1% year-over-year.

Developing... Check back for updates.

data.title: Oklo is the most overbought utilities stock as Q1 earnings roll on
data.description:
As the market moves into the second week of Q1 earnings, we look at the most overbought stocks in the utilities sector.

Below is a list of the top 10 utilities stocks ranked based on their Relative Strength Index as Q1 earnings season enters its second week. The list includes electric, multi-utility, water, and gas providers.

The list is topped by Oklo Inc. (OKLO), with an RSI of 66. NorthWestern Energy Group (NWE) and Black Hills Corporation (BKH) follow, with American States Water Company (AWR) and H2O America (HTO) rounding out the top five. Water utilities feature prominently in the rankings, while major players such as Entergy Corporation (ETR) and NiSource Inc. (NI) also appear as momentum remains steady across the sector.

The Relative Strength Index is a momentum oscillator that measures the velocity and magnitude of price changes, plotting them on a scale from 0 to 100. RSI compares the magnitude of recent gains to recent losses over a chosen lookback period, typically 14 days.

Here is the list:

  1. Oklo Inc. (OKLO), RSI: 66

  2. NorthWestern Energy Group, Inc. (NWE), RSI: 64

  3. Black Hills Corporation (BKH), RSI: 62

  4. American States Water Company (AWR), RSI: 61

  5. H2O America (HTO), RSI: 60

  6. Hawaiian Electric Industries, Inc. (HE), RSI: 60

  7. MDU Resources Group, Inc. (MDU), RSI: 59

  8. California Water Service Group (CWT), RSI: 59

  9. Entergy Corporation (ETR), RSI: 59

  10. NiSource Inc. (NI), RSI: 56

Utilities ETFs: (XLU), (VPU), (FUTY), (IDU), (FXU), and (JXI)

data.title: British retailer Primark will get dressed up for its public trading debut next year
data.description:
Associated British Foods (ASBFY) (ASBFF) is planning to demerge Primark into a separately listed company by the end of 2027. Following the business split, the food, and agriculture businesses will remain under the Associated British Foods name. The company expects the split will improve long-term shareholder returns by giving both businesses a clearer investment case and more focused governance.

Under the plan, shareholders would own stakes in both businesses after the separation. Primark would become a stand-alone retail group, while the food business would keep the ABF name and include brands such as Twinings, Ryvita, Patak’s, grocery, ingredients, agriculture, and sugar. 

The rationale for the split is that the current conglomerate structure obscures the value of both businesses. Separating Primark should let investors value it more like a pure-play apparel retailer, while the food business can be viewed more cleanly as an FTSE 100 food producer. The board also said the demerger is the best way to maximize long-term returns for shareholders. Looking ahead, recurring dis-synergies of less than £45M are anticipated, and one-off separation and transaction costs of about £75M.

Primark competes with other fast-fashion and value apparel retailers such as Zara, H&M (HNNMY), Shein (SHEIN), Uniqlo (FRCOY) (FRCOF), Next, Matalan, New Look, Marks & Spencer (MAKSF) (MAKSY), ASOS (ASOMF) (ASOMY), River Island, and Mango.
